questions.q: On what date was Sukhdev Thapar executed?
questions.explanation: Sukhdev was hanged on 23 March 1931 in Lahore.

questions.q: Sukhdev was executed alongside which two other revolutionaries?
questions.explanation: Sukhdev was hanged with Bhagat Singh and Rajguru on 23 March 1931.

questions.q: Sukhdev was a leading member of which revolutionary organization?
questions.explanation: Sukhdev was a leading member of the Hindustan Socialist Republican Association (HSRA).

questions.q: In which city was Sukhdev executed?
questions.explanation: Sukhdev was executed in Lahore Central Jail in 1931.

questions.q: What was Sukhdev's full name?
questions.explanation: His full name was Sukhdev Thapar.

questions.q: In which region did Sukhdev mainly help build the revolutionary network?
questions.explanation: Sukhdev was central to building the HSRA's revolutionary organization in Punjab.

questions.q: With which famous revolutionary was Sukhdev a close friend and associate?
questions.explanation: Sukhdev was a close associate and friend of Bhagat Singh.

questions.q: In which city was Sukhdev born?
questions.explanation: Sukhdev was born in Ludhiana in Punjab in 1907.

questions.q: In which conspiracy case was Sukhdev sentenced to death?
questions.explanation: Sukhdev was condemned to death in the Lahore Conspiracy Case.

questions.q: Roughly how old was Sukhdev when he was executed in 1931?
questions.explanation: Sukhdev, born in 1907, was about 23 years old when he was hanged.

questions.q: Which youth organization in Punjab did Sukhdev help organize alongside Bhagat Singh?
questions.explanation: Sukhdev was active in organizing the Naujawan Bharat Sabha to spread revolutionary ideas among Punjabi youth.

questions.q: At which Lahore college was Sukhdev a student involved in revolutionary activity?
questions.explanation: Sukhdev studied at the National College in Lahore, a hub of nationalist students including Bhagat Singh.

questions.q: In which year was Sukhdev born?
questions.explanation: Sukhdev was born on 15 May 1907.

questions.q: Sukhdev's organizational role in the HSRA was chiefly as what?
options: A recruiter and organizer of the Punjab network
questions.explanation: Sukhdev is remembered as a quiet but able organizer who built and managed the HSRA's Punjab network.

questions.q: The Saunders killing of 1928, linked to Sukhdev's group, avenged the death of which leader?
questions.explanation: The killing of Saunders by the HSRA avenged Lala Lajpat Rai, fatally injured during a Simon Commission protest.

questions.q: Which slogan did Sukhdev and his HSRA comrades raise against colonial rule?
questions.explanation: Sukhdev and his comrades raised the slogan 'Inquilab Zindabad', meaning 'Long Live the Revolution'.

questions.q: Which British police officer was killed in the 1928 Lahore action tied to Sukhdev's organization?
questions.explanation: Assistant Superintendent John Saunders was shot dead in Lahore in December 1928 by HSRA members.

questions.q: The reorganization of the revolutionary group in 1928 that Sukhdev supported added which word to its name?
questions.explanation: The Hindustan Republican Association was renamed the Hindustan Socialist Republican Association in 1928, adding 'Socialist'.

questions.q: Sukhdev belonged to which broad ideological strand of the freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: Sukhdev shared the HSRA's revolutionary outlook combined with socialist ideals for a free India.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is Ludhiana, Sukhdev's birthplace, located?
questions.explanation: Ludhiana is in the state of Punjab, where Sukhdev was born and active.

questions.q: Sukhdev's execution took place during which broader Gandhian campaign in 1931?
questions.explanation: The 1931 executions of Sukhdev and his comrades occurred during the Civil Disobedience era and provoked national outrage.

questions.q: Sukhdev is widely believed to have written a letter to which leader on the subject of the revolutionary path?
questions.explanation: A letter attributed to Sukhdev addressed to Gandhi debated the place of revolutionary methods in the freedom struggle.

questions.q: Which study circle or 'lab' is Sukhdev associated with running to educate young revolutionaries in Lahore?
options: A chemistry laboratory for explosives only
A printing press for the Kesari newspaper
A political training and study group for HSRA recruits
questions.explanation: Sukhdev helped run study and training circles in Lahore that schooled young recruits in revolutionary politics.

questions.q: The bodies of Sukhdev and his comrades were secretly cremated by the British near the bank of which river?
questions.explanation: The bodies were taken to the banks of the Sutlej near Hussainiwala for a hurried secret cremation.

questions.q: At which memorial near Ferozepur is Sukhdev honoured along with Bhagat Singh and Rajguru?
questions.explanation: The National Martyrs Memorial at Hussainiwala honours Sukhdev, Bhagat Singh and Rajguru.

questions.q: Sukhdev was implicated in the death of which informer associated with the HSRA, a charge raised at trial?
options: A police approver / informer connected to the group
questions.explanation: Sukhdev was tied at trial to the case involving the killing of an informer linked to the revolutionary circle, part of the wider conspiracy charges.

questions.q: Which honorific has Sukhdev's birthplace area used to commemorate him, alongside an annual observance?
options: Shaheed Sukhdev, marked on Martyrs' Day
questions.explanation: Sukhdev is commemorated as a shaheed (martyr), with 23 March observed as Martyrs' Day for the three revolutionaries.

questions.q: Sukhdev's family later settled in which city after Partition, where a memorial and his ashes are honoured?
questions.explanation: Sukhdev is commemorated in Ludhiana, his birthplace, where memorials honour his sacrifice.

questions.q: Sukhdev was part of the leadership that reorganized the revolutionary body at which 1928 Delhi gathering?
questions.explanation: The HSRA was constituted at a meeting at the Feroz Shah Kotla ruins in Delhi in 1928, with Sukhdev among the Punjab leaders.

questions.q: In which year did the Lahore Conspiracy Case proceedings that condemned Sukhdev conclude with death sentences?
questions.explanation: The Lahore Conspiracy Case tribunal handed down death sentences to Sukhdev, Bhagat Singh and Rajguru in 1930, carried out in March 1931.
